pr_description:
This PR contains some fixes needed to handle files with special characters correctly (#92):

- escape special characters file names in "flow coverage" commands (with the same regular expression used in the `flow-annotation-checks` dependency to fix the same issue).
- update flow-annotation-checks dependencies (which contains the fix for the "extract flow annotation" phase).
- added an additional integration test that execute `flow-coverage-report` on a fake project with special characters in the file names.

Thanks to @ryan953 for tracking down the issue and for fixing for it.
commit_message: fix: Escaped special chars in filenames (#92)

pr_description: This PR includes the changes from #130 (contributed by @runarberg) and applied some additional changes (to fix some issues related to the usage of the svgo dependency, tweak the badge generation and add a new integration test for the feature).

pr_description:

commit_message: fix: Added support for the new flow annotations (strict and strict-local)

pr_description:
This PR includes the changes proposed in #157 and adds an additional --percent-decimals cli option (which can also be set in a config file as percentDecimals) to optionally include decimals numbers in the computed percent values, and some additional unit and integration tests. 

Fix #148 feature request.

commit_message: chore: Updated svgson dev dependency to v4.0.0

pr_description:
Some dependencies have security problems so I updated all of the production ones (the ones that are important for projects using the lib). 

Because the project uses an old Flow version I had to hack a bit around the flow libdefs, but that should be OK. The dependencies don't have a lot of changes, so keeping the old libdefs was easy. Only for yargs I got it from flow-typed' previous version.

Because there was an issue with eslint in travis I also went ahead and upgraded both babel and eslint. And then fixed all new eslint violations. So this makes a big patch! but with quite independent commits. I hope that will be ok :-)

Next step could be to upgrade jest, that shouldn't be too painful, and then maybe you can add depfu.com (my favorite dependency upgrader) on this repository so that it's handled some more automatically and progressively in the future.

I checked with our project and this runs and gives the same result as before the change (both text and html) in about the same time.

One test seems flaky but it seemed flaky on master too (the one about the badge)... It looks like it passes on Travis though.

This PR adds an integration test suite (based on jest as the test units).
This tests are going to execute the final built file instead of testing the single modules, and they do not use any mock.
This PR contains fixes and new tests related to #57
commit_message: fix: Fixed total percent 0 rendered as NaN in report text

pr_description:
`summaryReportContent()` and `sourceReportContent()` implicitly know what `reportType` to use. So I removed the `reportType: opt.type` field from their props and let `html-report-page.jsx` export two components.

This unlocks the ability to specify specific flowtypes for Summary and SourceFile report pages, which we now also have. Should increase coverage, remove optional/unused fields and prevent that `threshold` issue from happening again.
commit_message:
Split the flow type for the two HTMLReport*Page components

The pages take slightly different info, while the header/footer is consistent. This splits up the types so we can refine them better.
